name: Pictou County Oatcakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the dry ingredients and cut in shortening.
2. Dissolve baking soda in boiling water and add to the mixture, continuing to mix with a knife.
3. Mold with your hands and shape into a long wedge.
4. Slice off pieces and bake at 400°F for 10 minutes.
This recipe comes from the county where the Scots first landed in Canada (and where my dad was born.) To quote the author, "Our Scottish ancestors used 'real' oatmeal when they made their favorite oatcakes. However sugar did creep in, as indicated by this 75 year old recipe. (The book was published in 1971 so the recipe would be from the year 1894.)"
